supply_chain.dependencies: Substantial amount of mail order and specialty pharmacies' prescription drug supply from a limited number of suppliers; many products manufactured outside of U.S.; certain agreements with suppliers are short-term and cancellable without cause; single source of supply for some products
risks: Supply chain disruptions from tariffs or trade restrictions could increase costs and impact product availability

Supply shortages; loss of single source suppliers; supplier production disruptions; supplier quality issues; closing or bankruptcies of suppliers; tariffs and trade policy changes; supplier concentration; dependence on pharmaceutical manufacturers for rebates and discounts

regulatory_risks: Telehealth licensing and regulatory requirements
Anti-corruption laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act)
Cybersecurity regulations
State and federal PBM regulations seeking to regulate pricing, rebates, pharmacy network practices and formulary management
CMS audits and risk adjustment data validation (RADV)
Federal and state anti-kickback statutes and false claims act exposure
FDA regulation of compounding pharmacies and medical devices
CMS regulations and audits of Medicare Advantage and Part D programs including RADV audits with potential for material premium refunds
Federal False Claims Act violations and qui tam lawsuits
Pharmacy pricing legislation and MAC price regulations
Telemarketing and consumer contact regulations
PBM regulation at federal and state levels
ERISA fiduciary duties and prohibited transactions
ACO and value-based care program regulations
Medicare star ratings requirements
DEA and state controlled substances regulations
Guaranty fund assessments for insolvent insurers in all states where Company operates
Pharmacy network access legislation and any willing provider laws
State insurance department regulations requiring maintenance of statutory capital and surplus levels
Medicaid regulatory changes and state budget constraints
Medicare Advantage and Part D regulatory changes
International data protection laws (GDPR)
340B drug pricing program regulations
FTC investigations and enforcement actions regarding PBM practices including rebate arrangements and pharmacy contracting
State privacy laws (CCPA and similar)
OFAC sanctions compliance
Guaranty fund assessments
Risk adjustment program requirements and potential payment adjustments under Medicare Advantage
Corporate practice of medicine restrictions
HIPAA and state privacy laws governing use and disclosure of personal health information
State false claims acts and anti-kickback statutes
Consumer protection laws and accessibility requirements
DEA and controlled substances regulations including opioid-related litigation and enforcement
ACA minimum medical loss ratio (MLR) requirements and potential rebate obligations
Anti-money laundering regulations
State Attorney General investigations of PBM pricing, rebates, and insurance practices
HIPAA privacy and security violations
Climate change and sustainability regulations
FDA approval requirements and healthcare regulatory compliance
Anti-kickback statute (AKS) violations
Premium rate review and approval processes
DOJ investigations of PBM practices, Medicare/Medicaid billing, and controlled substances dispensing
Stark Law violations
Formulary and plan design regulation
Provider network adequacy requirements and directory accuracy regulations
ACA compliance and potential changes or repeal
Environmental and safety regulation
State insurance and HMO regulation
Minimum MLR rebate requirements
Potential suspension or exclusion from government programs due to regulatory violations
Transparency in Coverage Rule requirements

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions based on U.S. foreign policy
Implementation of new tariffs could create exposure for increased costs and supply chain disruptions
